// Module
var code = "<h1 id=adding-a-model>Adding a Model</h1> <p>To add a model to an existing collection, you’ll need:</p> <ul> <li>A model file in <a href=https://www.khronos.org/gltf/ >glTF format</a></li> <li>A name to call it</li> </ul> <p>You can preview your exported glTF models using Don McCurdy’s online <a href=https://gltf-viewer.donmccurdy.com/ >glTF viewer</a>, which is powered by the same <a href=https://threejs.org/ >three.js</a> library as this site.</p> <h2 id=format>Format</h2> <p>Models must be in <a href=https://www.khronos.org/gltf/ >glTF format</a>. They may be <code>.gltf</code> or <code>.glb</code>, with or without compression. Binary (<code>.glb</code>) with compression is preferred for simplicity and reduced asset size. See <a href=./#Exporting-to-glTF>Exporting to glTF</a> for instructions for common modeling software.</p> <h2 id=code>Code</h2> <p>To add a model to an existing collection, you’ll need to add a new <code>Model</code> to the <code>App</code> definition for the collection. You should also add any new contributors to the collection to the <code>contributors</code> list.</p> <p><strong>collection.ts</strong></p> <div class=sourceCode id=cb1><pre class=\"sourceCode diff\"><code class=\"sourceCode diff\"><span id=cb1-1><a href=#cb1-1 aria-hidden=true></a>  import { App, Model } from &#39;../app&#39;</span>\n<span id=cb1-2><a href=#cb1-2 aria-hidden=true></a>  import pathToOtherModel from &#39;./Some other model.glb&#39;</span>\n<span id=cb1-3><a href=#cb1-3 aria-hidden=true></a><span class=va>+ import pathToModel from &#39;./My Model.glb&#39;</span></span>\n<span id=cb1-4><a href=#cb1-4 aria-hidden=true></a>  </span>\n<span id=cb1-5><a href=#cb1-5 aria-hidden=true></a>  new App({</span>\n<span id=cb1-6><a href=#cb1-6 aria-hidden=true></a>      title: &#39;My Collection&#39;,</span>\n<span id=cb1-7><a href=#cb1-7 aria-hidden=true></a>      groups: [</span>\n<span id=cb1-8><a href=#cb1-8 aria-hidden=true></a>          {</span>\n<span id=cb1-9><a href=#cb1-9 aria-hidden=true></a>              name: &#39;models&#39;,</span>\n<span id=cb1-10><a href=#cb1-10 aria-hidden=true></a>              items: [</span>\n<span id=cb1-11><a href=#cb1-11 aria-hidden=true></a>                  new Model({ name: &#39;Some other model&#39;, path: pathToOtherModel }),</span>\n<span id=cb1-12><a href=#cb1-12 aria-hidden=true></a><span class=va>+                 new Model({ name: &#39;My model&#39;, path: pathToModel }),</span></span>\n<span id=cb1-13><a href=#cb1-13 aria-hidden=true></a>              ]</span>\n<span id=cb1-14><a href=#cb1-14 aria-hidden=true></a>          },</span>\n<span id=cb1-15><a href=#cb1-15 aria-hidden=true></a>      ],</span>\n<span id=cb1-16><a href=#cb1-16 aria-hidden=true></a>      contributors: [</span>\n<span id=cb1-17><a href=#cb1-17 aria-hidden=true></a>          &#39;Wirt&#39;,</span>\n<span id=cb1-18><a href=#cb1-18 aria-hidden=true></a><span class=va>+         &#39;Jason Funderberker&#39;,</span></span>\n<span id=cb1-19><a href=#cb1-19 aria-hidden=true></a>      ]</span>\n<span id=cb1-20><a href=#cb1-20 aria-hidden=true></a>  })</span></code></pre></div> <p>What’s happening:</p> <ul> <li><p>Specify the model file (<code>My Model.glb</code>) as a dependency:</p> <div class=sourceCode id=cb2><pre class=\"sourceCode typescript\"><code class=\"sourceCode typescript\"><span id=cb2-1><a href=#cb2-1 aria-hidden=true></a><span class=im>import</span> pathToModel from <span class=st>&#39;./My Model.glb&#39;</span></span></code></pre></div> <p>The relative path to the file is returned in the variable <code>pathToModel</code>.</p></li> <li><p>Create a new <code>Model</code> instance:</p> <div class=sourceCode id=cb3><pre class=\"sourceCode typescript\"><code class=\"sourceCode typescript\"><span id=cb3-1><a href=#cb3-1 aria-hidden=true></a><span class=kw>new</span> <span class=fu>Model</span>({ name<span class=op>:</span> <span class=st>&#39;My model&#39;</span><span class=op>,</span> path<span class=op>:</span> pathToModel })</span></code></pre></div> <p><code>name</code> specifies the name used in the sidebar, which is also used to create the unique link to the item. <code>path</code> tells the code where to look for the model file.</p></li>
